contenders.handicapper_notes: Debutant by Ilovethiscity who won a recent Donald jump-out on July 16. John Rowe stable has a modest strike rate, but must respect the trial form.
Resumes. Ran a solid fourth first-up last campaign in a BM56 on soft turf. Won a recent trial at Bendigo. Wide draw is the main obstacle.
First-starter by Widden Valley. Runner-up in a Hamilton jump-out on July 13. Beautifully drawn in barrier 2 but this looks like a tough debut assignment.
Resuming. Showed promise last prep with a fourth on heavy ground at Sale. Trial winner at Mornington on July 15 suggests he is ready to fire first-up.
Debutant by Prince Of Caviar. Runner-up in a Hamilton jump-out on July 13. Trained by Tom Dabernig and gets the services of John Allen. Highly respected.
Fitter for a first-up fourth at Mildura over 1200m on a slow track. Has wet track experience and the Preusker stable is always dangerous here.
Debutant by Puissance De Lune. Runner-up in a Mortlake jump-out on June 30. Trained on the course, but others look more precocious.
Resumes. Sat on the speed and boxed on well for third on debut at Mornington on a soft track. Recent trial winner at Wangaratta. Drawn perfectly.
Debutant by Magnus out of Saffrossa. Runner-up in a Hamilton jump-out on July 13. Trained on the course by Symon Wilde. Expect a bold showing.
First-starter by Widden Valley. Finished fourth in a Hamilton jump-out on July 13. Hard to recommend from a wide gate on debut.
Debutant by I Am Invincible. Won a St Arnaud jump-out on July 6. Well-bred filly who commands respect if the market speaks favorably.
Tracked the speed and boxed on steadily for sixth on debut at Moe on a heavy track. Fitter now and can improve, but the draw is very tough.
